diagnosis: The level has a staircase of platform jumps leading up to the exit, but the player disengaged quickly (12.7s, no deaths, no falls) and rated it low, suggesting the jump sequence felt tedious or repetitive rather than genuinely challenging. With zero falls and fast abandonment, the difficulty wasn't the barrier — the pacing or monotony of jump-after-jump was the issue.
lessons: A long sequence of similar jumps in a row can feel tedious rather than exciting, even if each jump is easy; vary jump distances, heights, or add alternate paths to maintain engagement.
Fast completion times or quick disengagement with zero deaths/falls signals boredom or frustration with repetition, not difficulty — check level variety, not just challenge.
